experiment: F-BRN6 reverse direction: does P-creation predict subsequent domain expansion?

hypothesis.H_reverse: P-creation sessions predict +1.5-2x new-domain lanes in ±3 session window (bidirectional coupling)
hypothesis.complement: L-566 forward direction: 1.40x lift (domain-seeding → P-creation same-session)

method.domain_expansion_metric_1: First occurrence of new domain in session log (new-domain rate)

forward_correlation.note: domain variety in session S → P-creation in session S+1: weak negative

verdict.H_reverse: FALSIFIED — P-creation predicts LESS domain expansion (0.34x, inverted from 1.5-2x hypothesis)
verdict.direction: Anti-correlation: P-creation sessions cluster in consolidation mode (harvest/batch-scan), not domain expansion
verdict.interpretation: Neuroplasticity isomorphism is one-directional: domain exploration → principle extraction (L-566 1.40x). Principle extraction does NOT trigger domain exploration. Consistent with neuroscience: LTP consolidation happens during integration phases, which are anti-correlated with new-domain acquisition.

surprises: Direction is fully INVERTED (0.34x vs 1.5-2x expected) — not just null
P-creation sessions have 3x LOWER domain variety than non-P-creation sessions — consolidation and exploration are mutually exclusive modes
Forward correlation also slightly negative (r=-0.112): domain variety at time T weakly anti-predicts P-creation at T+1
Suggests two distinct session types: consolidation (principle extraction) vs exploration (domain DOMEX) that rarely co-occur
implications_for_brn6: F-BRN6 neuroplasticity isomorphism holds only in one direction (domain expansion → P-creation). Reverse direction falsified. This refines the isomorphism: swarm 'neuroplasticity' is triggered by domain contact, but principle consolidation (like LTP) happens in specialized consolidation sessions that suppress new domain exploration.
